authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1910.119 - Process Safety Management of Highly Hazardous Chemicals
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA's PSM standard is the primary U.S. rule for operating procedures, training, contractor controls, pre-startup review, maintenance, incident investigation, and hot-work permits at covered chemical processes.
used_for: boundary against engineering and safety-signoff overreach

authoritative_sources.title: Process Safety Management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA 3132 is authoritative operating guidance for how chemical facilities document operating procedures, safe work practices, training, contractor coordination, mechanical integrity, incident investigation, and compliance-audit records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Field evidence and work execution review

authoritative_sources.title: CPL 02-01-065 Process Safety Management of Highly Hazardous Chemicals
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This OSHA enforcement directive is authoritative for inspection workflow, record review, and what documentation chemical facilities must produce during PSM-focused oversight.

authoritative_sources.title: 40 CFR Part 68 - Chemical Accident Prevention Provisions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The current eCFR text is the primary federal rule for regulated-substance thresholds, operating procedures, training, maintenance, incident investigation, emergency response, and recordkeeping under EPA's Risk Management Program.
used_for: threshold and Program 2 or 3 applicability posture

authoritative_sources.title: Risk Management Program (RMP) Rule Overview
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA's RMP overview is authoritative public guidance for threshold-triggered accident prevention, emergency-response planning, and risk-management-plan duties at chemical facilities.

authoritative_sources.title: Guidance for Auditing Risk Management Plans/Programs Under Clean Air Act Section 112(r)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA's audit guidance is authoritative for how regulated facilities' RMP records, operating evidence, audit trails, and corrective actions are reviewed during oversight.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Audit, incident, and corrective-action evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: Recommended Practice 754 Fact Sheet
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: API is a recognized industry standards body, and RP 754 is a widely used public process-safety-indicator framework for classifying abnormal events and process-safety evidence relevant to chemical plant operations.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO/TC 251 Asset Management Overview
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO/TC 251 is the official ISO source for the ISO 55000 asset-management family, a recognized public standard framework for lifecycle asset records, maintenance knowledge, and risk-informed operating decisions.

workflow_stages.name: Accepted-work intake and prerequisite confirmation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm facility, unit, product, shift, work order, permit scope, authority map, and systems-of-record links before any lifecycle-state progression is attempted.
source_classes: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Operating-readiness and control review
workflow_stages.description: Check operating procedures, batch sheets, lineups, safe-work permits, operator handoffs, maintenance and calibration posture, and active hold conditions before work proceeds.
source_classes: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards

workflow_stages.name: Execution-state progression and in-process exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Use field rounds, DCS or event logs, lab samples, inspection findings, and work-order status to decide whether execution continues, holds, reworks, or escalates.
source_classes: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards
Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Completion confirmation and evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Require completion evidence such as final samples, cleanup verification, transfer or batch closeout, turnover records, and resolved exception posture before closure.
source_classes: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards
Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance

workflow_stages.name: Regulated-risk escalation and authority-side routing
workflow_stages.description: Escalate permit or threshold gaps, changed field conditions, environmental exceedances, incident posture, or requests for engineering, safety, or regulator-facing signoff that move authority outside the lane.
source_classes: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards

artifacts: operating procedures, startup and shutdown checklists, batch sheets, and line-up or line-clearance records
board-operator logs, shift turnover notes, field-round sheets, DCS event or alarm logs, and permit-board status
hot-work, confined-space, line-break, and lockout or isolation permits
lab samples, certificates of analysis, wastewater and emissions monitoring records, and off-spec or deviation logs
CMMS or EAM work orders, preventive-maintenance status, calibration certificates, asset-condition findings, and bad-actor histories
inspection findings, safety observations, incident notes, corrective-action trackers, and complaint or upset records
environmental reporting workpapers, permit conditions, threshold-applicability references, and audit or oversight records
completion evidence including cleanup verification, transfer confirmations, closed exceptions, and signed turnover records
systems_of_record: production execution or historian-linked operations log system

allowed: Classify chemical-plant operating work as in-scope, blocked, rework-required, ready to advance, or ready to document as complete based on named records.
Prepare evidence-backed execution updates, completion records, workflow summaries, and escalation notes tied to permits, samples, inspections, work orders, and operator records.
Request missing tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context before allowing lifecycle-state progression.
escalate: Permit, threshold, sample, inspection, calibration, or maintenance evidence is missing, stale, contradictory, or newly adverse.
Field conditions, environmental indicators, incident posture, or alarm and safeguard impairments materially change whether work may proceed or close.
Engineering, safety, legal, EHS, or regulator-facing ownership is required to resolve the request or conflict.
refuse: Requests for engineering approval, safety-critical proceed authority, legal interpretation, permit modification, reportability determination, or regulator-facing submission.
Requests to complete or continue work when required evidence, permits, samples, inspections, or approvals are missing or contradictory.
Requests that would bypass documented permit-to-work, maintenance hold, threshold, environmental, or incident-investigation controls.

domain_failure_modes: Advancing plant work based on generic status labels instead of actual batch, permit, sample, inspection, and asset-condition records.
Treating off-spec samples, analyzer drift, or equipment defects as minor notes rather than answer-changing execution exceptions.
Marking work complete without signed turnover, final sample, cleanup, inspection, or closure evidence.
Ignoring permit conditions, threshold posture, or environmental monitoring gaps when determining whether a unit can continue operating.
Collapsing changed field conditions into the existing ready state instead of reopening the lifecycle and escalating.
Drifting into engineering signoff, EHS judgment, maintenance workaround approval, or regulator-facing reporting because the task sits in an operations queue.
Using tenant SOP language to override stricter public-baseline requirements without explicit escalation and owner review.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Permit, threshold, or inspection evidence gap
Field condition that changes the risk posture
Request for engineering, safety, or authority-side signoff
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
Normal in-scope execution from accepted work to completion
Processing blocked by missing upstream data or approvals
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation that preserves the public baseline
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Facility-specific permit stack and jurisdiction mix
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Different facilities may be governed by different combinations of Title V, wastewater, hazardous-waste, state, or local permit programs. The package remains validated because runtime requires the tenant to supply the controlling permit set and jurisdiction before high-confidence operating decisions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Exact threshold applicability and connected-process edge cases
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public sources define threshold frameworks, but facility-specific substance concentrations, connected inventories, and covered-process determinations must be retrieved at runtime to decide exact PSM or RMP posture in edge cases.

notes: Authoritative plant-operation outputs depend on tenant-designated EHS, CMMS, work-order, permit, LIMS, incident, and document-control systems rather than generic assumptions.
The lane stops before engineering approval, safety-critical proceed authority, maintenance workaround approval, environmental certification, legal interpretation, and regulator-facing reporting.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for this validated package because the remaining ambiguities are explicit runtime prerequisites rather than gaps in the public baseline.
